titleStmt.title: Letter from Joseph Slevin to the Longford Urban District Council, [July 1916]
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Joseph Slevin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Joseph Slevin to the Longford Urban District Council in July 1916, concerning the drainage and sanitary arrangements for some houses in Longford. Joseph Slevin (b.1856) was the Town Surveyor for Longford. The Longford Urban District Council was the towns local authority and was renamed the Longford Town Council in 2002.

correspAction.placeName: Longford Urban District Council, Co. Longford, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Mr. Chairman & Gentlemen I inspected the drainage and sanitary
arrangements at the back of houses on
St. Joseph's Road, Mr. Farthy's house etc.
I found (a) the drain pipe not self-
cleaning (b) joints not pointed in cement.
(c) that the pipe has been broken in several
places and not sealed up afterwards (d)
where the pipes pass through the dwelling house
the floor has been broken and not properly
repaired. There seems to be no disconnecting trap
or proper ventilating shaft. The paving of yard
and channel is very open and bad, the closets
and pit are very near back doors. Fowl are
kept in boxes quite near each back door
and the place seems very congested Your obdt. servant Joseph Slevin
